verdict_reason: BG 2.63 sits in the sthita-prajña block (2.54-2.72), which Chapter 2's thesis identifies as 'the seasoned operator's state of mind — what they look like when functioning correctly'. BG 2.63's engineering analog supports this thesis by describing operator-state specifics: vulnerability of the experienced practitioner (2.60), the conjunction of restraint and orientation (2.61), the cascade of fall (2.62-2.63), the alternative serene engagement (2.64-2.65), and the no-shortcut closing (2.66). The voice is consistent with the chapter's didactic-corrective register. The verse builds progressively from the parallel-agent block A (2.54-2.59 — Arjuna's question + Krishna's first answer) and prepares for block C (2.67-2.72 — closing). Pass.
